message:
security(checkpoint): prevent path traversal in storage, export, integrity, and restore

Introduces comprehensive path-traversal protection across the entire
checkpoint subsystem. Previously, session names and checkpoint IDs were
used directly in filepath.Join calls without validation, meaning a
crafted session name like "../escape" or a malicious scrollback file
reference like "../../etc/passwd" could read or write files outside the
intended checkpoint directory tree.

Storage layer (storage.go):
- New safeSessionDir validates session names via tmux.ValidateSessionName
  before constructing the session directory path.
- New validateCheckpointID rejects empty IDs, dot-prefixed IDs, IDs
  containing ".." or path separators, and any ID not matching the
  allowlist regex [A-Za-z0-9._-]+.
- New safeCheckpointDir composes both validations and is now used by
  Save, Load, List, Delete, Exists, SaveGitPatch, LoadGitPatch, and
  SaveGitStatus — every method that constructs a checkpoint path.
- CheckpointDir (the public accessor) falls back to a sanitized path
  via safePathFallbackComponent if validation fails, ensuring callers
  never receive a path that escapes BaseDir.
- New resolveCheckpointRelativePath validates that a relative file
  reference (e.g. a scrollback filename or patch filename stored in
  checkpoint metadata) resolves to a path within the checkpoint
  directory. Uses filepath.Rel to detect ".." traversal after joining
  and cleaning the path.

Export layer (export.go):
- exportTarGz and exportZip now call resolveCheckpointRelativePath for
  each file entry instead of raw filepath.Join, preventing archive
  extraction from including files outside the checkpoint directory.

Integrity layer (integrity.go):
- checkFiles validates scrollback file paths and git patch file paths
  via resolveCheckpointRelativePath before checking existence or hashing.
- GenerateManifest similarly validates all file paths before hashing,
  returning errors for any path that escapes the checkpoint directory.

Restore layer (restore.go):
- ValidateCheckpoint now validates scrollback paths via
  resolveCheckpointRelativePath and reports "invalid scrollback path"
  issues instead of silently constructing traversal paths.

CLI layer (cli/checkpoint.go):
- listCheckpointSessions now reads from storage.BaseDir instead of
  hardcoding ~/.local/share/ntm/checkpoints, making it consistent with
  the Storage instance and testable with NewStorageWithDir.
- Results are now sorted for deterministic output.
- cli/checkpoint_test.go updated to call listCheckpointSessions(storage)
  instead of the removed listCheckpointSessionsWithDir helper.

Test coverage additions:
- checkpoint_test.go: TestStorage_LoadRejectsInvalidIdentifiers verifies
  that "../escape", ".", and similar IDs are rejected by Load.
- restore_test.go: TestValidateCheckpoint_RejectsScrollbackTraversal
  confirms that "../../etc/passwd" in a ScrollbackFile field produces
  an "invalid scrollback path" validation issue.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
